title: Bar and catering supervisors
description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in preparing and serving food. Prepare and cook large quantities of food for institutions, such as schools, hospitals, or cafeterias. Prepare meals in private homes. Includes personal chefs. (Based on 15 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Prepare tables for meals, including setting up items such as linens, silverware, and glassware, and escort customers to their tables.

Check patrons' identification to ensure that they meet minimum age requirements for consumption of alcoholic beverages and attempt to limit problems and liability related to customers' excessive drinking by taking steps such as persuading customers to stop drinking or ordering taxis for intoxicated patrons.

Clean or sterilize dishes, kitchen utensils, equipment, or facilities, and perform cleaning duties, such as sweeping and mopping floors, vacuuming carpet, tidying up server station, and taking out the trash.
Examine trays to ensure they contain required items, and load trays with accessories, such as eating utensils, napkins, or condiments.
Place food servings on plates or trays according to orders or instructions, and serve food or beverages to patrons, preparing or serving specialty dishes at tables as required.
Assist host or hostess by answering phones to take reservations or orders, and by greeting, seating, and thanking guests, while providing information about local areas, including directions.

Take food orders and relay orders to kitchens or serving counters, stock service stations with items, and write patrons' food orders on order slips, memorize orders, or enter orders into computers for transmittal to kitchen staff.
Monitor food preparation or serving techniques to ensure that proper procedures are followed, and prepare food items, such as sandwiches, salads, soups, or beverages.
Remove dishes and glasses from tables or counters and take them to the kitchen for cleaning, along with cleaning tables or counters after patrons have finished dining.
Serve meals and snacks to employing families and their guests, and collect payments from customers, ensuring accuracy in transactions.
Prepare checks that itemize and total meal costs and sales taxes, and take inventory of supplies and equipment.
Ensure food is stored and cooked at the correct temperature by regulating the temperature of ovens, broilers, grills, and roasters, while monitoring and recording food temperatures for safety.
Maintain kitchen work areas, equipment, or utensils in clean and orderly condition, and clean and sanitize work areas, equipment, utensils, dishes, or silverware.
Prepare a variety of foods according to customers' orders or supervisors' instructions, including baking, cooking meats, and preparing vegetables.

Monitor food distribution, ensuring that meals are delivered to the correct recipients and that guidelines, such as those for special diets, are followed, while determining where patients or patrons would like to eat their meals.
Train new employees and supervise the work of kitchen staff, coordinating and directing activities during meal service.
Prepare meals in private homes according to employers' recipes or tastes, and plan menus according to employers' needs and diet restrictions.
Create drink recipes and prepare mixed drinks for patrons, including garnishing and decorating drinks.
Stock bar with beer, wine, liquor, and other related supplies, ensuring all items are adequately stocked and the bar area is clean and attractive.
Check with customers to ensure they are enjoying their meals and take action to correct any problems that arise during dining.
Provide customers with detailed descriptions of menu items and recommendations upon request, while monitoring customer satisfaction.
Collect and record amounts and types of special food items served to customers, maintaining accurate records of food use.
Carry food, silverware, or linen on trays or use carts to carry trays, and assist in loading dishes, glasses, and tableware into dishwashing machines.
Prepare and serve a variety of beverages, including both alcoholic and non-alcoholic options, and explain daily specials to patrons.
Remove trash and clean kitchen garbage containers, ensuring the sanitation of the work environment.
Prepare and package individual place settings, arranging them for ease of service.
Load or unload trucks that deliver or pick up food or supplies as necessary, ensuring all deliveries are checked and processed correctly.
Plan and prepare food for parties, holiday meals, luncheons, special functions, and other social events, offering creative and diverse menu options.
Set up banquet tables and organize dining areas for large parties, ensuring proper arrangements and settings.
Butcher and dress animals, fowl, or shellfish, and prepare them for cooking or serving, ensuring all food is handled safely and correctly.
Weigh, measure, and mix ingredients according to recipes or personal judgment, ensuring food is prepared correctly and efficiently.
Supervise the work of bar staff and other bartenders, ensuring all operations run smoothly.
Keep records pertaining to menus, finances, and other business-related issues, contributing to efficient management.

Mix ingredients, such as liquor, soda, water, sugar, and bitters, to prepare cocktails and other drinks; serve wine and bottled or draft beer; and prepare or serve hot or cold beverages, such as coffee, espresso drinks, blended coffees, or teas.

Clean and organize eating, service, and kitchen areas, including performing cleaning duties such as sweeping, mopping, washing dishes, and scrubbing and polishing counters, steam tables, and other equipment.

Plan, prepare, and deliver meals to individuals with special dietary needs, assessing nutritional needs of patients, planning special menus, supervising the assembly of diet trays, and overseeing the delivery of food trolleys to hospital patients.
Take customers' orders, write ordered items on tickets, give ticket stubs to customers when needed, and communicate with customers regarding orders, comments, and complaints.
Set up dining areas for meals, arrange tables and decorations according to instructions, and stock customer service stations with paper products or beverage preparation items.
Restock kitchen supplies, rotate food, monitor and order supplies or food items, keep inventory, and ensure efficient service through supply inspections.
Deliver orders to kitchens, pick up and serve food when it is ready, and carry food, dishes, trays, or silverware from kitchens or supply departments to serving counters.
Observe and evaluate workers and work procedures to ensure quality standards and service, and resolve customer complaints regarding food service.
Prepare large quantities of food, cook simple foods and beverages, and perform various food preparation tasks, such as making sandwiches or carving meats.
Greet and seat customers, present menus and wine lists, and collect and return dirty dishes to the kitchen for washing.
Provide training to workers in food preparation, service, sanitation, and safety procedures, and supervise the assembly of regular and special diet trays.
Clean food preparation equipment, work areas, and counters or tables, and maintain adequate supplies of items, such as clean linens, silverware, glassware, or dishes.
Accept payment from customers, make change as necessary, run cash registers, balance receipts and payments, and compile and balance cash receipts at the end of the shift.

Perform personnel actions, such as hiring and firing staff, providing employee orientation and training, and conducting supervisory activities, such as creating work schedules or organizing employee time sheets.
Perform general cleaning activities in kitchen and dining areas to ensure a sanitary environment for food preparation and service.
Train new staff in operational procedures, including food preparation and cleanliness standards, and monitor their adherence to these practices.
Demonstrate the use of retail equipment, such as espresso machines, and perform food preparation and serving duties such as carving meat or serving wine and liquor.

detailed_work_activities.title: Stock serving stations or dining areas with food or supplies.
Clean food preparation areas, facilities, or equipment.
Store supplies or goods in kitchens or storage areas.
Prepare foods for cooking or serving.
Present food or beverage information or menus to customers.
Move equipment, supplies or food to required locations.
Monitor food services operations to ensure procedures are followed.
Collect dirty dishes or other tableware.
Communicate dining or order details to kitchen personnel.
Maintain food, beverage, or equipment inventories.
Communicate with customers to resolve complaints or ensure satisfaction.
Coordinate activities of food service staff.
Check quality of foods or supplies.
Train food preparation or food service personnel.
Inspect facilities, equipment or supplies to ensure conformance to standards.
Manage food service operations or parts of operations.
Provide customers with general information or assistance.
Estimate supplies, ingredients, or staff requirements for food preparation activities.
Assist chefs or caterers with food or drink preparation.
Create new recipes or food presentations.
Report information to managers or other personnel.
Coordinate timing of food production activities.
Distribute resources to patrons or employees.
Evaluate quality of materials or products.
Manage preparation of special meals or diets.
Usher patrons to seats or exits.
Assist customers to ensure comfort or safety.

emerging_tasks.task: Check with customers to see if they want to apply any rewards to their purchase.
Perform routine tasks, such as refilling syrups, sanitizer bottles, and other essential supplies.
Check and log refrigerator, freezer, and cooler temperatures.
Clean and sanitize the dining area, including tables.
Provide customers with directions or answers to questions.
Check and monitor food temperatures while cooking.
Prepare food, such as sandwiches, for customers.

alternate_titles: Back of House Team Member (BOH Team Member)
